steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Orphan Repair · Theory Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7280 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Orphan Repair contract for the supplied data.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to expose PostgreSQL and S3 crash windows through an exhaustive workflow-state result; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Orphan Repair model · Theory Orphan Repair, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Deleting every unreferenced key found in one list pass is safe garbage collection. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
